_inspectorSnowReport.Report:
3/7/26 11:30 AM Update: Chair 6 is now open!
Well, what was supposed to be a bit of a stormy day has turned out quite mellow with little to no precip and now spots of sunshine around the mountain! With warm overnight temps, anticipate the skiing to start soft and springy both on and off groomers. Come on up and surf the slush! If you need to drop your skis off at the Tune Shop, today would be the day to get a fresh wax and give your skis a little bit of TLC before hitting the slopes. Thank you for continuing to respect all terrain closures, riding in control, and bringing the stoke for the mountains. Discovery and Quicksilver run until 7pm for the second to last weekend for night skiing. Can't wait to see you up here today!
 
Terrain Parks: The Discovery Park, Arwine's Jump Line and the new Sudden Drop Park (Lower Arwine's) are open today!

Events: 
  • On Sunday March 8th, join us for Get the Girls Out. This event cultivates a community for women to have fun, connect with new and old friends and creates opportunities. 
  • The Campbell Basin Rail Jam has moved to next Saturday March 14th!  From 11am-2pm throw down your cleanest lines, stomp your best tricks, and compete for some seriously sweet prizes from our stacked sponsors. Not competing? No stress — pull up, hype your crew, and catch all the action from Campbell Basin Lodge (elite viewing vibes included).
Notes for today:
  1. Summit House will be closed March 11 and Rafters will be closed March 11 and 12 for private events. 
  2. $199 Spring 3-Day Passes are on sale now! Ski for just $67 a day, any day of the week, for the rest of the season!
  3. $62 Day tickets continue every Tuesday for the remainder of the season!
  4. Did you know it just takes 5 Days to be entered to win a 26/27 Ikon Pass? Track your days on the Ikon App and register for RIDE TO WIN
  5. Looking to save time and make the most of the day? Consider grabbing a Daily Reserve Upgrade! Available on weekends and holidays, this upgrade grants access to priority lift lines, getting you on the lift faster and allowing you to complete more laps. 
  6. Paid reservations can only be cancelled by emailing SP+ at  CrystalMtnParking@spplus.com with Reservation #, First Name, and Last Name. While you may not receive an immediate reply, the cancellation will be processed. The Crystal Mountain Contact Center cannot process these cancellations.
  7. In case of on-mountain emergency: Call 360-663-3064 or 911

Mountain Weather 3/7/2026 4:20pm 

Forecaster: Mike Haft  
  
Synopsis:   
An offshore ridge finally gets pancaked and opens the flood gates for a booming week of continuous winter weather.  In the short term, we find ourselves on the front end of a slow cooling trend bringing Freezing levels below the base area by Monday morning.  We will still need to endure Sunday’s moist wrath as light liquid precipitation will likely fall at all elevations during the day.  We can count on Big Tahoma to once again save us from complete deluge as Paradise is set to take the better part of 1.00” of water compared to less than 0.25” of water at Crystal.      
  
Saturday Night:    
Sky Cover: Cloudy w/ chance of very light rain 
Freezing Elevation: 7500’ and falling  
12hr Precip: Little to none  
Wind Trends: Strong WSW 
Sunday:   
Sky Cover: Mostly Cloudy w/ Light mixed precip  
Freezing Elevation: 6500’ and falling 
12hr Precip: Less than 0.25” water 
Wind Trends: Strong WSW  
Sunday Night:    
Sky Cover: Mostly Cloudy  
Freezing Elevation: 2500’  
12hr Precip: 1” possible 
Wind Trends: Moderate to Strong W  
  
Extended:   
Cool Westerly flow settles in with ample moisture to fling daily snow showers our way through Tuesday.  The main event takes stage Tuesday afternoon and rages through the extended.  Fair Warning, the gram and other social media feeds are throwing out some wild numbers, so reign it in and let the forecast unfold and come to consensus.  Snow is coming, it will be cold enough, but just as Big Tahoma protects us from rain, she also steals our snow.  This week’s weather looks powerful and will certainly deliver the bam bam.  

*Daily Summaries: 12hr period representing 4am-4pm or 4pm-4am data
*Freezing Elevation (ft): Approximate elevation above sea level where the air temperature reaches 32 degree F

Uphill Travel Update: See the above banner for the current status. Please visit guest services to get your 25/26 uphill travel card. This must be renewed free of charge for the season. Uphill travel may be prohibited at any time as mountain operations teams conduct avalanche mitigation. Refer to the "No Uphill Travel" lights around the base area and the Mountain Report page indicating the status of the designated route. These lights are located by the Gondola trail map, at the bottom of Chinook, at the bottom of Discovery, and at the top of the Quicksilver lift shack. For more information about uphill travel, please visit the uphill travel page. 

25/26 WINTER PROGRAMS, CLINICS, AND NECESSITIES

  • Ski and Ride School: Explore our programs and find the right fit for you.
  • AIARE Course & Certification: Ski the backcountry with expert knowledge and certification
